rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports or features.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid and use only visible symbols from the model scope.
Assumptions should reflect data constraints with concrete bounds or named constants.
Guarantees should relate outputs to inputs using visible variables.
Avoid using implementation-specific subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the actual data types of the referenced variables.
Range assumptions should use helper declarations like `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`.
Do not reference AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time directly in AGREE expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not generate clauses that reference opaque Boolean data as integers.
Do not assume that AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
Model analyst extraction of component structure
